The Hong Kong Dental Association also said on Wednesday a poll of local practitioners found that each dentist needed to follow up on an average of four cases a month relating to treatment received across the border.
The Health Bureau and the Department of Health said in a joint statement....

articles.title: India’s ‘cockroach’ protests: a rare political opening for the opposition?
articles.summary:
India’s student-led “cockroach” protests, which began as anger over alleged entrance exam leaks, are now becoming a broader test of whether Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s government can contain young Indians’ frustration over education, jobs and accountability.
The fast-growing movement gained political momentum on Tuesday when opposition leader Rahul Gandhi of the Congress party and his sister Priyanka Gandhi Vadra were forcibly removed after a gathering with about 100 fellow lawmakers outside....

articles.title: US levels AI sanctions threat as China models gain
articles.summary:
US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent signalled potential action against open source artificial intelligence (AI) models amid accusations that Chinese developers are creating low-cost systems using stolen US intellectual property.
“This administration supports open source models, but what we do not support is IP theft,” Bessent said on Fox Business on Tuesday. “If we see, especially, that overseas models are stealing from our great companies, we have the ability to sanction them.”

articles.title: China’s cyberscam crackdown pushed syndicates to target Americans, analysts say
articles.summary:
Criminal syndicates increasingly turned from targeting Chinese nationals to Americans after Beijing’s 2023 crackdown on online scams in Southeast Asia, analysts said on Wednesday, triggering a global game of “whack-a-mole”.
“China has a huge advantage and a head start in terms of trying to protect its own citizens from scams compared to the US,” said Sue-Lin Wong, who hosts the Scam Inc. podcast, adding that it is “terrible for Americans who are losing a lot of money”.
In recent years, Southeast...

articles.title: Daniel Siad, French modelling scout with links to Epstein found dead
articles.summary:
A French modelling scout with close ties to convicted US sex offender Jeffrey Epstein has been found dead at his home outside Paris, prosecutors and his lawyer said on Wednesday.
Under investigation in France, Daniel Siad, 69, was one of several French men accused of aiding the US financier to traffic and abuse women.
Siad had not yet been questioned by investigators, but had denied the allegations.
“An investigation to determine the cause of death was opened on Monday evening following the...

articles.title: China, EU pushing ahead on joint research despite trade tensions
articles.summary:
China and the EU have renewed their joint research agreement, according to Chinese state-linked media, signalling a willingness to collaborate on climate and agriculture despite trade tensions.
People’s Financial News, a publication affiliated with Communist Party mouthpiece People’s Daily, reported on the new two-year agreement on Wednesday.
China’s Ministry of Science and Technology and the European Commission had signed joint research funding agreements to support scientific cooperation...

articles.title: Philippines’ impeachment trial: court ruling heightens stakes for VP Sara
articles.summary:
Philippine Vice-President Sara Duterte-Carpio suffered a serious setback in her impeachment trial this week after the Senate allowed prosecutors to examine key financial records in an unexplained wealth case against her.
Analysts said the Senate impeachment court’s ruling could give prosecutors a clearer route to show she amassed ill-gotten wealth and pull down her popularity ratings in time for the 2028 presidential election, thus raising the stakes for Duterte-Carpio as she fights not only to....

articles.title: Why China’s polar expedition to the Arctic Ocean has drawn US ire
articles.summary:
The United States Coast Guard said it closely tracked Chinese polar expedition vessels transiting the Bering Sea on their way to the Arctic Ocean last week, amid unease over Beijing’s geopolitical ambitions in the region.
The US Coast Guard Cutter Munro actively monitored the Chinese research ships Xuelong and Xuelong 2 as they passed northbound through the US exclusive economic zone (EEZ) and extended continental shelf in the Bering Sea towards the Arctic, the US Coast Guard said last...

articles.title: Malaysia won’t be intimidated over Israelis’ entry ban, Anwar tells US lawmakers
articles.summary:
Malaysia would not be intimidated into changing its decades-old policy of not allowing Israeli nationals into the country, Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim said on Wednesday.
Anwar said Malaysia was also not in contravention of any international laws for having such a policy in place.
“I would like to respond to the United States lawmakers, some of them at least, who stated categorically that Malaysia is in contravention of international laws and was disrespectful of the rights of nations.
“This is....

articles.title: 4 boys arrested over wet tissue ball, water assaults on pedestrians in Tsuen Wan
articles.summary:
Hong Kong police have arrested four boys as young as 12 years old after they allegedly threw wet tissue balls and splashed water at passers-by while cycling along Tsuen Wan’s waterfront and in a public park.
Tsuen Wan Chief Inspector Leung Wai-yee said on Wednesday that the arrests followed the circulation of videos online showing the incidents that took place between July 18 and 20.
The four boys, aged 12 to 14, were arrested in Tsuen Wan and Kwai Tsing on suspicion of common assault.
The Form....

articles.title: World’s first AI model to earn perfect score at maths Olympiad comes from China’s RedNote
articles.summary:
For the first time, an artificial intelligence model has achieved a perfect score at the International Mathematical Olympiad (IMO) – and it was developed by Chinese social giant RedNote.
The model, dots-note-3.0, solved all six puzzles and secured a perfect score of 42 at this year’s IMO, which concluded in Shanghai on Monday.
The note variant, still in beta phase, is currently the lightest model in the dots3 family, which includes two larger versions – jazz and aria – tailored for different use...

articles.title: Russia’s grinding war in Ukraine shows the impotence of nuclear weapons
articles.summary:
Why haven’t Ukraine’s drone and missile attacks on Russia triggered Moscow’s use of tactical nuclear weapons, as it has threatened? When Russia is attacked time and again, one wonders what purpose it serves to have the world’s largest nuclear arsenal.
The Ukraine war is at a stalemate. Though roughly 90 per cent of the territory in East Donbas is under Russian control, its ground advances have slowed to less than 100 metres a day, according to a July 1 report by the Centre for Strategic...

articles.title: Despite regulatory crackdown, Chinese investors look to Hong Kong, US to diversify
articles.summary:
Around half of Chinese investors with assets over 1 million yuan (US$147,703) plan to increase their investments in Hong Kong and the United States over the next 12 months to diversify their portfolios, despite Beijing’s crackdown on cross-border brokerages, according to a new survey.
The findings come after the DBS Treasures Affluent Investor Survey polled 1,617 individuals in Hong Kong and mainland China with at least HK$1 million (US$127,525) or 1 million yuan in assets in their respective...

articles.title: Hong Kong loses 2030 maths congress to Glasgow. Does Asia need a stronger voice?
articles.summary:
An award-winning mathematician has called for Asia to set up its own maths union after Hong Kong lost out to Glasgow to host a top international meeting in the field.
Tsinghua University professor Shing‑Tung Yau, a member of the Hong Kong bid committee, made the call after he said the International Mathematical Union (IMU) awarded the hosting rights for the 2030 International Congress of Mathematicians (ICM) to the British city on Tuesday night.
“My colleagues and I are deeply regretful about...

articles.title: Congo’s Ebola outbreak kills nearly 1,000 people, warning of worse to come
articles.summary:
At least 999 people have died from the Ebola outbreak in Congo, according to official data published overnight into Wednesday, a grim toll in what has already been declared the fastest Ebola outbreak in history.
The latest update from Congo’s Ministry of Health shows that as of Monday, 2,473 cases have been recorded in the latest outbreak, and 999 people have died. At least 737 patients were currently in isolation and hospitals, it said.
The outbreak declared on May 15 is unlike most previous...

articles.title: Beijing and Manila should avoid South China Sea ‘miscalculation’: top diplomats
articles.summary:
Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi met his Philippine counterpart in Manila on Wednesday, with both sides highlighting the need for “reducing the risk of miscalculation” and maintaining stability in the South China Sea.
The meeting on the sidelines of the Asean foreign ministers’ gathering marked the first formal talks between the top diplomats since Maria Theresa Lazaro assumed office in July last year.

articles.title: Japanese agency warns of ‘cruelly hot days’ amid 40 degrees and above heat
articles.summary:
Japanese cities are experiencing “cruelly hot days”, a new meteorological term devised this year to warn people of intense heat above 40 degrees Celsius (104 Fahrenheit), with emergency officials describing the soaring temperatures on Wednesday as “a disaster”.
Heatstroke alerts were issued across almost the whole country and emergency officials said at least 14 people had died from heat-related causes in the past week.

Chinese regulators moved on Tuesday to improve the quality of credit ratings for panda bonds – a yuan-denominated asset class that has seen a surge in interest from foreign sovereign and institutional investors this year as a key tool to bolster Beijing’s yuan-internationalisation push.

articles.summary:

When Chaiwat Kovavisarach first stepped into Bangchak Corporation Plc in the early 2000s, the Thai oil refiner was fighting for survival.
The aftershocks of the 1997 Asian financial crisis were still reverberating through Thailand’s economy. Several global banks had attempted to stabilise it without success.
